skill.name: Financial Projections
skill.description: Create comprehensive 3-5 year financial models with revenue projections, cost structures, headcount planning, cash flow analysis, and three-scenario modeling for startup financial planning and fundraising.
skill.summary: Build detailed 3-5 year financial models with revenue, costs, cash flow, and scenario analysis for startups.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis found zero security issues. This skill provides text-based financial modeling guidance and templates for Claude to follow when building financial projections.

content.value_statement: Build comprehensive 3-5 year financial models with revenue projections, cost structures, and scenario analysis for startup planning and fundraising.
actual_capabilities: Build cohort-based revenue projections over 3-5 years
Create detailed cost structures including COGS, S&M, R&D, and G&A
Plan headcount by role with compensation and equity
Calculate monthly cash flow and runway
Compute key metrics including CAC, LTV, burn rate, and efficiency ratios
limitations: Does not execute financial calculations - provides guidance only
Cannot access real-time market data or benchmarks
Does not integrate with external financial software
User must validate assumptions and outputs

use_cases.title: Fundraising Preparation
use_cases.description: Create investor-ready financial projections showing 3-5 year revenue, costs, and capital requirements for pitch decks and due diligence.
use_cases.target_user: Founders preparing for VC pitch or seed round

use_cases.title: Board Meeting Reporting
use_cases.description: Build detailed financial models to present to board members with scenario analysis and key metric tracking.
use_cases.target_user: CFOs and finance leads at startups

use_cases.title: Budget and Hiring Planning
use_cases.description: Develop headcount plans and departmental budgets aligned with revenue growth targets and runway considerations.
use_cases.target_user: Startup operators planning growth and resource allocation

prompt_templates.title: Basic Financial Projection Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 3-year financial projection for my [SaaS/Marketplace/E-commerce] startup. We currently have $[X] MRR with [Y] customers. Expect [X]% monthly growth with [Y]% churn. Our target ACV is $[X].
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick projection request with basic inputs

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Model with Scenarios
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete 5-year financial model with three scenarios (conservative, base, optimistic). Include revenue projections by cohort, detailed cost structure, headcount plan, cash flow analysis, and key metrics like CAC, LTV, burn rate, and runway.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full financial model with all components

prompt_templates.title: Fundraising Financial Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Create investor-ready financial projections for a $[X] seed round. Include use of proceeds, milestones, and how the funding extends runway. Show scenario analysis with different growth rates.

prompt_templates.title: Runway and Burn Rate Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate our current runway and project burn rate over the next 18 months. Show how different scenarios (slowing growth, faster growth, cutting costs) affect cash position.

output_examples.input: Create financial projections for B2B SaaS startup, $30K MRR, 50 customers, 8 person team, $400K cash, 20% MoM growth, 8% monthly churn, $600 ACV
output_examples.output: Comprehensive financial model with monthly revenue projections (Year 1-2), quarterly (Year 3), annual (Years 4-5), cost breakdown by department, headcount plan, cash flow table showing 18-month runway, key metrics dashboard (CAC $480, LTV $6,000, LTV:CAC 12.5), and three-scenario comparison table.

output_examples.input: Build 5-year model for Series A prep, include fundraising scenario with $5M raise at $20M valuation
output_examples.output: Detailed 5-year financial model with funding milestones, use of proceeds breakdown, post-money valuation scenarios, board-ready metrics dashboard including Rule of 40, burn multiple, magic number, and detailed scenario analysis showing conservative/base/optimistic outcomes.

best_practices: Use cohort-based revenue tracking to accurately model retention and expansion revenue over time
Always include three scenarios (conservative, base, optimistic) to show range of outcomes to stakeholders
Document all assumptions clearly and validate against industry benchmarks before finalizing projections
anti_patterns: Using flat revenue growth instead of modeling customer acquisition and churn explicitly
Underestimating fully-loaded employee costs (salary × 1.3-1.4 for benefits and equity)
Ignoring cash timing and payment terms when calculating runway

faq.question: How detailed are the financial projections?
faq.answer: The model provides monthly detail for Years 1-2, quarterly for Year 3, and annual for Years 4-5, with comprehensive tables for revenue, costs, headcount, and cash flow.

faq.question: What business models does this support?
faq.answer: This skill supports SaaS, marketplace, transaction-based, and e-commerce business models with customizable revenue and cost assumptions.

faq.question: Can I export to Excel or Google Sheets?
faq.answer: The skill generates markdown output with clear tables. You can copy these into spreadsheet software or use the provided formulas to build your own models.

faq.question: How do scenario assumptions work?
faq.answer: Conservative assumes -30% customers, +20% churn, -15% pricing. Optimistic assumes +30% customers, -20% churn, +15% pricing. Base is your most likely case.

faq.question: What key metrics are calculated?
faq.answer: The model calculates CAC, LTV, LTV:CAC ratio, CAC payback period, burn multiple, magic number, Rule of 40, monthly burn rate, and runway.

faq.question: How often should I update the projections?
faq.answer: Update monthly to track actual vs. projected performance. Update significantly when major assumptions change or before fundraising events.
